semanticCore: Burdening, oppressing, fatiguing, making bent, or weighing down
quranicRelevance: It does not burden/tire Him (Ya'ooduhu - 'wa-la ya'ooduhu').

keyDerivations.meaning: To burden, fatigue, tire, oppress, or weigh down with heavy load

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا (2:255): His Throne extends over the heavens and the earth, and preserving them does not tire/burden Him (divine omnipotence).
summaryEn: The root signifies that which tires or oppresses (Awd), describing God's effortless preservation of the entire cosmos without exhaustion or burden.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الثقل والمشقة والجهد الذي يثقل كاهل الحامل ويحنيه (أود)، نافياً أي تعب أو مشقة في حفظ الله السماوات والأرض ورعايتهما.
rawText:
أَوِدٌ ; (T, M;) or 🡻آوَدُ; fem. أَوْدَآءُ; (K;) Crooked, curved, or bent. (T, M, K.) أَوِدَ 1 , aor. يَأْوَدُ, inf. n. أَوَدٌ, It (a thing, T, S, M, or an arrow, AHn, M) was, or became, of itself, crooked, curved, or bent. (T, S, M, A, * K.) [See also 5.] ___ آدَ, aor. يَؤُودُ, inf. n. أَوْدٌ, It (the day) receded, in the evening. (T, L.) === It (the evening, T, S) declined. (T, S, K.) === It (a thing, L) returned. (M, L, K.) === آدَتِ الظِّلَالِ The shadows returned, and inclined towards the east. (L.) === آدَ عَلَيْهِ He inclined towards him; or pitied him. (M.) ___ آدَهُ, (T, S, Msb,) first pers. أُدْتُهُ, (M,) or أُدتُّهُ, (K, TA, [in the CK, erroneously, اِدتُّهُ,]) aor. يَؤُودُ, inf. n. أَوْدٌ, (As, T, M, Msb,) He crooked, curved, or bent, it; (As, T, S, L, Msb, K;) i. e., a stick, (As, T, L,) or other thing; (L;) as also 🡻أوّدهُ. (L, K.) === آدَهُ, aor. يَؤُودُ, (T, S, M, &c.,) inf. n. أَوْدٌ (S, M, K) and أُوُودٌ, (M, K,) It (a load) oppressed him by its weight; pressed heavily upon him; burdened him. (AZ, T, S, A, Msb.) And It (a thing, or an affair,) oppressed, distressed, or afflicted, him: (M, L, K:) and [in like manner] 🡻تأوّدهُ, (L, K,) or 🡻تآودهُ, (T,) as also تآداهُ, (L, K,) the last formed by transposition (T, L) from the second, (T,) or first, (L,) said of an affair, it pressed heavily upon him; oppressed him. (T, L, K.) You say, مَا آدَكَ فَهْوَ لِى 🡻آئِدٌ What hath burdened [or distressed] thee, it (that thing) is burdening [or distressing] to me. (S.) انآد 7 : see 5, in two places. === Also He became oppressed, or burdened [by a load]. (Msb.) اَوَّدَ 2 see 1. آئِدٌ Burdening [or distressing]. (S.) See 1, last sentence. آوَدُ ; fem. أَوْدَآءُ: see what next precedes. تأوّد 5 It (a stick, T, L, or some other thing, L) became, by an extraneous operation, crooked, curved, or bent; (T, S, M, A, L, K;) as also 🡻انآد. (T, S, M, L, K: [in the CK فَأْتادَ is erroneously put for فَٱنْآدَ.]) El-'Ajjáj says,

لَمْ يَكُ يَنْآدُ فَأَمْسَى ٱنْآدَا

[He used not to become bent, and he has become bent], making the pret. to be a denotative of state because قَدْ is meant to be understood, as in the saying in the Kur [iv. 92], أَوْ جَاؤُوكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ. (S.) You say also, تَأَوَّدَتْ فِى قِيَامِهَا She (a woman) bent in her rising, by reason of her heaviness. (T and L in art. وأد.) ___ : see آدَهُ. تآودهُ 6 : see آدَهُ. مَؤُودٌ Oppressed, pressed heavily upon, or burdened, by a load. (S.) مَآوِدُ Calamities: (IAar, M, L, K:) as also مَوَائِدُ, which is app. formed by transposition. (M, L.) Some say that مآود is pl. of مُؤْيِدٌ, and derive this word [which see in art. ايد] from آدَهُ, aor. يَؤُودٌ, meaning “ it oppressed him by its weight: ” (T, L:) or it has no sing. (IAar, M.)
